channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Atomically tweaking spin-crossover cooperativity to augment molecular memory density
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 23 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68796-4

Spin-crossover complexes are promising candidates for molecular bits owing to their magnetic bistability but engineering high-density bits is challenging due to cooperative switching dynamics. Here the authors modulate the cooperative dynamics of surface-confined spin-crossover complex chains.
item.dc:title: Atomically tweaking spin-crossover cooperativity to augment molecular memory density
item.dc:creator: Jing Liu
Yuchen Bai
Zhen Xu
Qiwei Chen
Jinliang Pan
Yuxuan Lin
Ting Hu
Haoyang Deng
Yifan Gao
Li Huang
Yongfeng Wang
Kai Wu

item.title: Polar discontinuities, emergent conductivity, and critical twist-angle-dependent behaviour at wafer-bonded ferroelectric interfaces
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 23 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68553-7

Wafer bonding has allowed the synthesis of twisted interfaces which support polar discontinuities in ferroelectric lithium niobate. Two-dimensional sheet conductivity arises but is suppressed when twist angles lead to interfacial lattice aperiodicity.
item.dc:title: Polar discontinuities, emergent conductivity, and critical twist-angle-dependent behaviour at wafer-bonded ferroelectric interfaces
item.dc:creator: Andrew Rogers
